water-saved.rationale: Public Works manages recycled water infrastructure, irrigation systems, and water conservation programs (~15% of dept budget)

trees-cared.rationale: Urban forestry, tree planting (~250/year), and Tree City USA maintenance (~10% of dept budget)

litter-collected.rationale: Street sweeping, public area cleanup, and Athens Services contract oversight (~5% of dept budget)

park-hours.rationale: Park maintenance, recreation programs, and volunteer coordination (~8% of dept budget)

water-saved.source: Metropolitan Water District of Southern California Tier 1 wholesale rate

trees-cared.source: USDA Forest Service average annual urban tree maintenance cost

litter-collected.source: Keep America Beautiful national average cleanup cost per pound

park-hours.source: Independent Sector estimated value of volunteer time (2023)

notes: Budget share percentages are estimates based on departmental program allocations in the FY 2025-26 Adopted Budget. Actual allocations may vary by line item.
